function_v0.doc: Toolchain / deploy sanity check: returns ["Hello", <to>].
function_v0.name: hello

function_v0.doc:
Deposit USDC into a tranche; mint pTokens to the user.

Mirrors `contracts/programs/prism-core/src/instructions/deposit.rs`.
First deposit mints 1:1 (NAV = 1.0). Subsequent deposits compute
shares = amount \xc3\x97 Q_ONE / nav_per_share_q.
function_v0.name: deposit

function_v0.doc:
Burn pTokens; pay out USDC at current NAV.

Mirrors `contracts/programs/prism-core/src/instructions/withdraw.rs`.
Allowed when vault is Active or Defaulted (so LPs can exit a written-down tranche).
Returns the USDC amount paid out.
function_v0.name: withdraw

function_v0.doc:
Originate a loan against a vault. Admin-only. No USDC moves yet \xe2\x80\x94
disbursement happens via `disburse_loan` (so admins can verify
off-chain collateral before unlocking funds, even though the IKA
collateral feature itself was dropped).

Mirrors `contracts/programs/prism-core/src/instructions/initialize_loan.rs`.
function_v0.name: init_loan

function_v0.doc: Create a new credit vault.\nAdmin-only. Vault starts in `Active` state with zero balances.
function_v0.name: init_vault

function_v0.doc:
Borrower repays USDC against a loan. State flips to `Repaid` once
`total_repaid >= principal`.

Mirrors `contracts/programs/prism-core/src/instructions/repay_loan.rs`.
function_v0.name: repay_loan

function_v0.doc: Initialize protocol global config. Single-shot; subsequent calls error.
function_v0.name: init_config

function_v0.doc:
Distribute USDC yield across the three tranches using the waterfall:
Prime fills its time-weighted target APY first, Core next, Alpha takes the residual.

Mirrors `contracts/programs/prism-core/src/instructions/accrue_yield.rs`.
Caller must be `config.admin` or in `oracle_allowlist`. `payer` is the
borrower-ish address that ships the USDC into the contract (kept
explicit so the auth flow is clear on Soroban).
function_v0.name: accrue_yield

function_v0.doc:
Create a tranche (Prime=0, Core=1, Alpha=2) inside an existing vault.
Admin-only. The `ptoken` argument is the Stellar Asset Contract address
of the pre-deployed pTranche token; this contract must be its admin so
it can mint/burn on deposit/withdraw (Phase 2).
function_v0.name: init_tranche

function_v0.doc: Transfer contract admin rights to a new address.\nRequires the current admin's auth. Irreversible without the new admin's key.
function_v0.name: update_admin

function_v0.doc:
Disburse a loan: contract sends USDC from its reserve to the borrower.
Admin-only. Loan must be in `Originated` state and vault must be `Active`.

Mirrors `contracts/programs/prism-core/src/instructions/disburse_loan.rs`,
minus the IKA collateral gate (cut for v1).
function_v0.name: disburse_loan

function_v0.doc:
Register a PRISM Collateral Oracle pubkey for a loan. Creates a
`CollateralRecord` in `Pending` state. The oracle_pubkey must be in
`config.oracle_allowlist`. This does NOT disburse or lock anything \xe2\x80\x94
disburse_loan remains blocked until `verify_collateral` succeeds.
function_v0.name: attach_collateral

function_v0.doc:
Oracle attests collateral is locked (status byte 0x01). Advances record
from Pending \xe2\x86\x92 Attached. After this call `disburse_loan` is unblocked.

Attestation message layout (73 bytes, \xc2\xa76.6):
bytes  0..8    b"col_atts"
bytes  8..12   loan_id (u32 LE)
bytes 12..16   chain_id (u32 LE)
bytes 16..48   asset_address (32 bytes)
bytes 48..56   amount_usd_micro (u64 LE)
bytes 56..64   valued_at_ts (i64 LE)
bytes 64..72   nonce (u64 LE)
byte  72       status (must be 0x01 for verify_collateral)
function_v0.name: verify_collateral

function_v0.doc: Oracle attests collateral is released (status byte 0x02). Advances\nrecord from Attached \xe2\x86\x92 Released. Called on full loan repayment.
function_v0.name: release_collateral

function_v0.doc:
Record a Cloak batch payout. The oracle attests that a batch of yield
has been shielded into Cloak's privacy pool and fanned out to LPs.
Verification is purely informational \xe2\x80\x94 it doesn't move USDC on-chain.

Attestation message layout (73 bytes):
bytes 0..8    "clk_atts"     prefix
bytes 8..40   vault_id_padded (u32 LE, zero-padded to 32 bytes)
bytes 40..72  batch_id (sha256 of off-chain disbursement receipt)
byte 72       result (0x01 = batch confirmed)

The signing pubkey must be in `config.oracle_allowlist`.
function_v0.name: record_cloak_payout

function_v0.doc:
Seed initial USDC + pToken liquidity into a Soroswap pool for one tranche.

Admin-only. Call once per tranche after the vault has been funded with
deposits and the Soroswap pair has been created (via the Soroswap factory
frontend). The core contract is the LP \xe2\x80\x94 it approves the router, calls
`add_liquidity`, and receives LP tokens back to its own address.

`usdc_min` and `ptoken_min` are slippage guards forwarded to Soroswap;
pass 0 for initial seeding where no pool price exists yet.

Returns `(usdc_used, ptoken_used, lp_minted)`.
function_v0.name: seed_pool_liquidity

function_v0.doc:
Borrower registers a sha256 commitment of their Encrypt-sealed credit
data, plus the pubkey of the oracle that will later sign default proofs.

`encrypt_oracle` must be in `config.oracle_allowlist`. The pubkey is
stored on the per-loan EncryptLoanHealth record and re-validated on
every `verify_encrypt_default` call.

Mirrors `contracts/programs/prism-core/src/instructions/attach_encrypt_score.rs`.
function_v0.name: attach_encrypt_score

function_v0.doc: Admin-triggered liquidation: oracle attests status byte 0x03 and the\nloss cascade fires for `loss_amount` against the vault's tranches.
function_v0.name: liquidate_collateral

function_v0.doc:
Read the most recent Reflector oracle price for a given asset symbol.

`reflector` is the Reflector oracle contract address.
`asset_symbol` is the ticker string, e.g. "BTC", "ETH", "USDC".

This is a simulation target \xe2\x80\x94 call via `simulateTransaction` from the
frontend to display mark-to-market collateral prices without a tx fee.
Returns `None` if Reflector has no data for the asset.
function_v0.name: read_reflector_price

function_v0.doc:
Reverse waterfall: Alpha absorbs first, then Core, then Prime.
On `Default`, vault state flips to `Defaulted`.

Mirrors `contracts/programs/prism-core/src/instructions/trigger_credit_event.rs`.
`loan_id` is recorded on the event but no loan-state mutation happens here.
function_v0.name: trigger_credit_event

function_v0.doc: Lightweight check for operations tooling.
function_v0.name: is_oracle_allowlisted

function_v0.doc:
Verify an Encrypt FHE default attestation and fire the loss cascade.

Attestation message layout (73 bytes), byte-identical to the Solana
version so the off-chain oracle can produce one message for either
chain by just swapping the loan-identifier semantics:
bytes 0..8    "enc_atts"     prefix
bytes 8..40   loan_id_padded (loan_id as u32 LE, zero-padded to 32 bytes)
bytes 40..72  score_commitment (must match attach time)
byte 72       result (0x01 = default proven)

On valid signature + valid message:
1. EncryptLoanHealth \xe2\x86\x92 DefaultProven
2. Reverse-waterfall cascade (Alpha \xe2\x86\x92 Core \xe2\x86\x92 Prime) for `loss_amount`
3. Vault \xe2\x86\x92 Defaulted, credit_event_seq++
function_v0.name: verify_encrypt_default

function_v0.doc: Add an oracle pubkey to the global allowlist.\n\nAdmin-only. Fails if the key is already allowlisted or the list is full.
function_v0.name: add_oracle_to_allowlist

function_v0.doc: Return the cumulative USDC absorbed by the loss cascade for a vault.\nTogether with \xce\xa3 tranche.total_assets, it equals the contract's USDC\nreserve: reserve == \xce\xa3 tranche.total_assets + loss_bucket_balance.
function_v0.name: get_loss_bucket_balance

function_v0.doc: Atomically replace one allowlisted oracle pubkey with another.\n\nAdmin-only. Useful for key rotation without a two-transaction gap.
function_v0.name: rotate_oracle_allowlist_key

function_v0.doc: Remove an oracle pubkey from the global allowlist.\n\nAdmin-only. Fails if the key is not currently allowlisted.
function_v0.name: remove_oracle_from_allowlist

fields.doc: Q64.64 fixed-point NAV per share. See [math::Q64_ONE].
fields.name: nav_per_share_q

fields.doc: Stellar Asset Contract address of the pTranche token (mint/burn handle).
fields.name: ptoken

fields.doc: Up to 8 oracle pubkeys (Ed25519 public keys, 32 bytes each).\nUsed to validate Encrypt and Cloak attestations.
fields.name: oracle_allowlist

udt_struct_v0.doc:
73-byte attestation message layout (\xc2\xa76.6 of stellar-migration-plan.md):
bytes  0..8    b"col_atts"
bytes  8..12   loan_id (u32 LE)
bytes 12..16   chain_id (u32 LE)
bytes 16..48   asset_address (32 bytes)
bytes 48..56   amount_usd_micro (u64 LE)
bytes 56..64   valued_at_ts (i64 LE)
bytes 64..72   nonce (u64 LE)
byte  72       status (0x01=Attached, 0x02=Released, 0x03=Liquidated)
udt_struct_v0.name: CollateralRecord

fields.doc: Ed25519 pubkey of the PRISM Collateral Oracle authorised for this loan.
fields.name: oracle_pubkey

void_v0.doc: Registered via attach_collateral but not yet oracle-verified.
void_v0.name: Pending

void_v0.doc: Oracle attested status=0x01: collateral locked, disburse allowed.
void_v0.name: Attached

void_v0.doc: Oracle attested status=0x02: collateral released on full repayment.
void_v0.name: Released

void_v0.doc: Oracle attested status=0x03: collateral liquidated, loss cascade fired.
void_v0.name: Liquidated

fields.doc: Ed25519 pubkey of the Encrypt oracle that signs attestations.
fields.name: encrypt_oracle

fields.doc: sha256 commitment of the borrower's Encrypt-sealed credit data.
fields.name: score_commitment

udt_error_enum_v0.doc: Mirrors the Anchor PrismError variants from the Solana implementation.\nCodes are sequential starting at 1 (Soroban convention) rather than 6000 (Anchor).\nThe variant *names* are preserved so existing docs and oracle messages remain meaningful.
udt_error_enum_v0.name: PrismError

cases.doc: attach_collateral has not been called for this loan.
cases.name: CollateralNotAttached

cases.doc: Collateral already verified (status != Pending); cannot re-verify.
cases.name: CollateralAlreadyVerified

cases.doc: Status byte in attestation message doesn't match the expected transition.
cases.name: CollateralStatusMismatch

cases.doc: Attestation message is malformed (wrong length or wrong prefix).
cases.name: CollateralInvalidMessage

cases.doc: Nonce in attestation has already been used (replay protection).
cases.name: CollateralNonceReused

cases.doc: disburse_loan blocked: collateral record exists but not yet verified.
cases.name: CollateralNotVerified

tuple_v0.doc: u128 tracking cumulative USDC absorbed by the loss cascade for this vault.\nMaintains: reserve == \xce\xa3 tranche.total_assets + loss_bucket_balance.
tuple_v0.name: LossBucketBalance

void_v0.doc: Monotonic counter for loan ids issued by this contract.
void_v0.name: NextLoanId

tuple_v0.doc: Monotonic counter for cloak payout records per vault.
tuple_v0.name: NextCloakSeq

tuple_v0.doc: PRISM Collateral Oracle record, keyed by loan_id.
tuple_v0.name: Collateral

udt_union_v0.doc:
Identifies an asset in the Reflector oracle.

`Stellar(addr)` is a Soroban token (SAC or SEP-41) identified by its contract address.
`Other(sym)` is a non-Stellar asset identified by its ticker symbol ("BTC", "ETH", etc.).
udt_union_v0.name: Asset

udt_struct_v0.doc: A price observation from the oracle.
udt_struct_v0.name: PriceData

fields.doc: Price in the oracle's base asset (usually USDC), scaled by `10^decimals()`.
fields.name: price
